NAME

crypto_handshake_context_t g_crypto_ctx = {0}

Global H.265 encoder context for client video streaming.

Detailed Description

🌐 Client connection manager: TCP connection, reconnection with exponential backoff, and thread-safe transmission

The connection management follows a robust state machine:

1.
Initialization: Socket creation and address resolution
2.
Connection: TCP handshake with configurable timeout
3.
Capability Exchange: Send terminal capabilities and client info
4.
Active Monitoring: Health checks and keepalive management
5.
Disconnection: Graceful or forced connection teardown
6.
Reconnection: Exponential backoff retry logic

Thread Safety

All packet transmission functions use a global send mutex to prevent interleaved packets on the wire. The socket file descriptor is protected with atomic operations for thread-safe access across multiple threads.

Reconnection Strategy

Implements exponential backoff with jitter:

  • Initial delay: 10ms
  • Exponential growth: delay = 10ms + (200ms * attempt)
  • Maximum delay: 5 seconds
  • Jitter: Small random component to prevent thundering herd

Platform Compatibility

Uses platform abstraction layer for:

  • Socket creation and management (Winsock vs BSD sockets)
  • Network error handling and error string conversion
  • Address resolution and connection timeout handling
  • Socket options (keepalive, nodelay)

Integration Points

  • main.c: Calls connection establishment and monitoring functions
  • protocol.c: Uses thread-safe send functions for packet transmission
  • keepalive.c: Monitors connection health and triggers reconnection
  • capture.c: Sends media data through connection
  • audio.c: Sends audio data through connection

Error Handling

Connection errors are classified into:

  • Temporary: Network congestion, temporary DNS failures (retry)
  • Permanent: Invalid address, authentication failure (report and exit)
  • Timeout: Connection establishment timeout (retry with backoff)
  • Loss: Existing connection broken (immediate reconnection attempt)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

2.0

Function Documentation

void server_connection_cleanup ()

Cleanup connection management subsystem. Closes any active connection and destroys synchronization objects. Called during client shutdown.

Definition at line 1106 of file src/client/server.c.

1107   if (!GET_OPTION(quiet)) {
1108     log_set_terminal_output(true);
1109   }
1110   server_connection_close();
1111   mutex_destroy(&g_send_mutex);
1112 }

void
  server_connection_close ()


Close the server connection gracefully. Close server connection
    gracefully.




Marks connection as inactive and closes the socket. This function
    is safe to call multiple times and from multiple threads.


Definition at line 955 of file
  src/client/server.c.


955                                {
956   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_close() called");
957 
958   // Acquire send mutex to prevent race with threaded_send_image_frame()
959   // This ensures any in-flight sends complete before we destroy the transport
960   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
961   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
962 
963   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
964   if (g_client_transport) {
965     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Destroying transport=%p (is_connected=%s) before closing socket",
966               (void *)g_client_transport, acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport) ? "true" : "false");
967     acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
968     g_client_transport = NULL;
969     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Transport destroyed, g_client_transport set to NULL");
970   }
971 
972   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
973 
974   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
975     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Closing socket: %d", (int)g_sockfd);
976     close_socket(g_sockfd);
977     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
978     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Socket closed");
979   }
980 
981   g_my_client_id = 0;
982 
983   // Cleanup crypto context if encryption was enabled
984   if (g_encryption_enabled) {
985     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Cleaning up crypto context");
986     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
987     g_encryption_enabled = false;
988   }
989 
990   // Turn ON terminal logging when connection is closed (unless it was disabled with --quiet)
991   if (!GET_OPTION(quiet)) {
992     log_set_terminal_output(true);
993   }
994   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_close() completed");
995 }

int
  server_connection_establish (const char * address, int port, int
  reconnect_attempt, bool first_connection, bool
  has_ever_connected)


Establish connection to ascii-chat server. Attempts to connect to
    the specified server with full capability negotiation. Implements
    reconnection logic with exponential backoff for failed attempts. On
    successful connection, performs initial handshake including terminal
    capabilities and client join protocol.




Parameters


address Server IP address or hostname


port Server port number


reconnect_attempt Current reconnection attempt number (0 for first)


first_connection True if this is the initial connection attempt


has_ever_connected True if a connection was ever successfully
  established


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 353 of file
  src/client/server.c.


354                                                          {
355   (void)first_connection; // Currently unused
356   if (!address || port <= 0) {
357     log_error("Invalid address or port parameters");
358     return -1;
359   }
360 
361   // Close any existing connection
362   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
363     close_socket(g_sockfd);
364     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
365   }
366 
367   // Apply reconnection delay if this is a retry
368   if (reconnect_attempt > 0) {
369     unsigned int delay_us = get_reconnect_delay(reconnect_attempt);
370     // Reconnection attempt logged only to file
371     platform_sleep_us(delay_us);
372 
373     // Check if user requested exit during reconnection delay
374     if (should_exit()) {
375       log_debug("Exit requested during reconnection delay");
376       return -1;
377     }
378   } else {
379     // Initial connection logged only to file
380   }
381 
382   // Check for WebSocket URL - handle separately from TCP
383   if (url_is_websocket(address)) {
384     // WebSocket connection - bypass TCP socket creation
385     // Use the original address (URL already contains port if specified)
386     const char *ws_url = address;
387 
388     // Parse for debug logging
389     url_parts_t url_parts = {0};
390     if (url_parse(address, &url_parts) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
391       log_info("Connecting via WebSocket: %s (scheme=%s, host=%s, port=%d)", ws_url, url_parts.scheme, url_parts.host,
392                url_parts.port);
393 
394       // Set server IP and port for crypto handshake
395       // This ensures the crypto context has proper server address info for known_hosts verification
396       server_connection_set_ip(url_parts.host);
397       log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Set server IP to %s from WebSocket URL", url_parts.host);
398     } else {
399       log_info("Connecting via WebSocket: %s", ws_url);
400     }
401 
402     // Initialize crypto if encryption is enabled
403     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_init()");
404     if (client_crypto_init() != 0) {
405       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto (password required or incorrect)");
406       log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_init() failed");
407       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
408       return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
409     }
410 
411     // Get crypto context for transport
412     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
413 
414     // Create WebSocket transport (handles connection internally)
415     g_client_transport = acip_websocket_client_transport_create("client", ws_url, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
416     if (!g_client_transport) {
417       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
418       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
419       return -1;
420     }
421     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Created WebSocket ACIP transport with crypto context");
422 
423     // Set connection as active
424     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
425     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
426 
427     // CRITICAL: Wait for WebSocket connection to be established before sending
428     // The service thread is running but may not have completed TLS handshake yet
429     // Don't block indefinitely - use timeout loops to allow responsiveness
430     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Waiting for WebSocket connection to be established...");
431     const int CONNECT_CHECK_ATTEMPTS = 300; // 300 * 50ms = 15 seconds timeout
432     int connect_attempts = 0;
433     while (!acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport) && connect_attempts < CONNECT_CHECK_ATTEMPTS) {
434       platform_sleep_us(50000); // Sleep 50ms between checks
435       connect_attempts++;
436       if (connect_attempts % 20 == 0) { // Log every 1 second (20 * 50ms)
437         log_info("CLIENT_CONNECT: Waiting for connection... (%d seconds elapsed)", connect_attempts / 20);
438       }
439     }
440 
441     if (!acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport)) {
442       log_error("Failed to establish WebSocket connection within 15 seconds");
443       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
444       g_client_transport = NULL;
445       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
446       atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
447       return -1;
448     }
449 
450     log_info("CLIENT_CONNECT: WebSocket connection established, proceeding with handshake");
451 
452     // Send initial terminal capabilities to server
453     int result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
454                                                               (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
455     if (result < 0) {
456       log_error("Failed to send initial capabilities to server: %s", network_error_string());
457       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
458       g_client_transport = NULL;
459       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
460       return -1;
461     }
462 
463     // Disable terminal logging after initial setup (for non-snapshot mode)
464     if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && has_ever_connected) {
465       log_set_terminal_output(false);
466     }
467 
468     // Build capabilities flags
469     uint32_t my_capabilities = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO;
470     log_debug("GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) = %d (sending CLIENT_JOIN)", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
471     if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
472       log_debug("Adding CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO to capabilities");
473       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO;
474     }
475     if (GET_OPTION(color_mode) != COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
476       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_COLOR;
477     }
478     if (GET_OPTION(stretch)) {
479       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
480     }
481 
482     // Generate display name from username + PID
483     const char *display_name = platform_get_username();
484     char my_display_name[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
485     int pid = getpid();
486     SAFE_SNPRINTF(my_display_name, sizeof(my_display_name), "%s-%d", display_name, pid);
487     if (threaded_send_client_join_packet(my_display_name, my_capabilities) < 0) {
488       log_error("Failed to send client join packet: %s", network_error_string());
489       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
490       g_client_transport = NULL;
491       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
492       return -1;
493     }
494 
495     log_info("WebSocket connection established successfully");
496     url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
497     return 0;
498   }
499 
500   // Resolve server address using getaddrinfo() for IPv4/IPv6 support
501   // Special handling for localhost: ensure we try both IPv6 (::1) and IPv4 (127.0.0.1)
502   // Many systems only map "localhost" to 127.0.0.1 in /etc/hosts
503   bool is_localhost = (strcmp(address, "localhost") == 0 || is_localhost_ipv4(address) || is_localhost_ipv6(address));
504 
505   struct addrinfo hints, *res = NULL, *addr_iter;
506   memset(&hints, 0, sizeof(hints));
507   hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC; // Allow IPv4 or IPv6
508   hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_STREAM;
509   if (is_localhost) {
510     hints.ai_flags = AI_NUMERICSERV; // Optimize for localhost
511   }
512 
513   char port_str[16];
514   SAFE_SNPRINTF(port_str, sizeof(port_str), "%d", port);
515 
516   // For localhost, try IPv6 loopback (::1) first, then fall back to DNS resolution
517   if (is_localhost) {
518     log_debug("Localhost detected - trying IPv6 loopback [::1]:%s first...", port_str);
519     hints.ai_family = AF_INET6;
520     hints.ai_flags = AI_NUMERICHOST | AI_NUMERICSERV;
521 
522     int ipv6_result = getaddrinfo("::1", port_str, &hints, &res);
523     if (ipv6_result == 0 && res != NULL) {
524       // Try IPv6 loopback connection
525       g_sockfd = socket_create("ipv6", res->ai_family, res->ai_socktype, res->ai_protocol);
526       if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
527         log_debug("Trying IPv6 loopback connection to [::1]:%s...", port_str);
528         if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, res->ai_addr, res->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
529           log_debug("Connection successful using IPv6 loopback");
530           SAFE_STRNCPY(g_server_ip, "::1", sizeof(g_server_ip));
531           freeaddrinfo(res);
532           res = NULL; // Prevent double-free at connection_success label
533           goto connection_success;
534         }
535         if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
536           log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
537         } else {
538           // log_debug("IPv6 loopback connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
539         }
540         close_socket(g_sockfd);
541         g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
542       }
543       freeaddrinfo(res);
544       res = NULL;
545     }
546 
547     // Check if user requested exit (Ctrl-C) before trying IPv4
548     if (should_exit()) {
549       log_debug("Exit requested during connection attempt");
550       return -1;
551     }
552 
553     // IPv6 failed, try IPv4 loopback (127.0.0.1)
554     log_debug("IPv6 failed, trying IPv4 loopback 127.0.0.1:%s...", port_str);
555     hints.ai_family = AF_INET;
556 
557     int ipv4_result = getaddrinfo("127.0.0.1", port_str, &hints, &res);
558     if (ipv4_result == 0 && res != NULL) {
559       g_sockfd = socket_create("ipv4", res->ai_family, res->ai_socktype, res->ai_protocol);
560       if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
561         log_debug("Trying IPv4 loopback connection to 127.0.0.1:%s...", port_str);
562         if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, res->ai_addr, res->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
563           log_debug("Connection successful using IPv4 loopback");
564           SAFE_STRNCPY(g_server_ip, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(g_server_ip));
565           freeaddrinfo(res);
566           res = NULL; // Prevent double-free at connection_success label
567           goto connection_success;
568         }
569         if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
570           log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
571         } else {
572           // log_debug("IPv4 loopback connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
573         }
574         close_socket(g_sockfd);
575         g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
576       }
577       freeaddrinfo(res);
578       res = NULL;
579     }
580 
581     // Both IPv6 and IPv4 loopback failed for localhost
582     log_warn("Could not connect to localhost using either IPv6 or IPv4 loopback");
583     return -1;
584   }
585 
586   // For non-localhost addresses, use standard resolution
587   log_debug("Resolving server address '%s' port %s...", address, port_str);
588   hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC;
589   hints.ai_flags = 0;
590   int getaddr_result = getaddrinfo(address, port_str, &hints, &res);
591   if (getaddr_result != 0) {
592     log_error("Failed to resolve server address '%s': %s", address, gai_strerror(getaddr_result));
593     return -1;
594   }
595 
596   // Try each address returned by getaddrinfo() until one succeeds
597   // Prefer IPv6 over IPv4: try IPv6 addresses first, then fall back to IPv4
598   for (int address_family = AF_INET6; address_family >= AF_INET; address_family -= (AF_INET6 - AF_INET)) {
599     for (addr_iter = res; addr_iter != NULL; addr_iter = addr_iter->ai_next) {
600       // Skip addresses that don't match current pass (IPv6 first, then IPv4)
601       if (addr_iter->ai_family != address_family) {
602         continue;
603       }
604 
605       // Create socket with appropriate address family
606       const char *socket_name =
607           (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6) ? "client_socket_server_ipv6" : "client_socket_server_ipv4";
608       g_sockfd = socket_create(socket_name, addr_iter->ai_family, addr_iter->ai_socktype, addr_iter->ai_protocol);
609       if (g_sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
610         log_debug("Could not create socket for address family %d: %s", addr_iter->ai_family, network_error_string());
611         continue; // Try next address
612       }
613 
614       // Log which address family we're trying
615       if (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET) {
616         log_debug("Trying IPv4 connection...");
617       } else if (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6) {
618         log_debug("Trying IPv6 connection...");
619       }
620 
621       // Attempt connection with timeout
622       if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, addr_iter->ai_addr, addr_iter->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
623         // Connection successful!
624         log_debug("Connection successful using %s", addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET    ? "IPv4"
625                                                     : addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6 ? "IPv6"
626                                                                                        : "unknown protocol");
627 
628         // Extract server IP address for known_hosts
629         if (format_ip_address(addr_iter->ai_family, addr_iter->ai_addr, g_server_ip, sizeof(g_server_ip)) ==
630             ASCIICHAT_OK) {
631           log_debug("Resolved server IP: %s", g_server_ip);
632         } else {
633           log_warn("Failed to format server IP address");
634         }
635 
636         goto connection_success; // Break out of both loops
637       }
638 
639       // Connection failed - close socket and try next address
640       if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
641         log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
642       } else {
643         // log_debug("Connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
644       }
645       close_socket(g_sockfd);
646       g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
647     }
648   }
649 
650 connection_success:
651 
652   if (res) {
653     freeaddrinfo(res);
654   }
655 
656   // If we exhausted all addresses without success, fail
657   if (g_sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
658     log_warn("Could not connect to server %s:%d (tried all addresses)", address, port);
659     return -1;
660   }
661 
662   // Connection successful - extract local port for client ID
663   struct sockaddr_storage local_addr = {0};
664   socklen_t addr_len = sizeof(local_addr);
665   if (getsockname(g_sockfd, (struct sockaddr *)&local_addr, &addr_len) == -1) {
666     log_error("Failed to get local socket address: %s", network_error_string());
667     close_socket(g_sockfd);
668     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
669     return -1;
670   }
671 
672   // Extract port from either IPv4 or IPv6 address
673   int local_port = 0;
674   if (((struct sockaddr *)&local_addr)->sa_family == AF_INET) {
675     local_port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(((struct sockaddr_in *)&local_addr)->sin_port);
676   } else if (((struct sockaddr *)&local_addr)->sa_family == AF_INET6) {
677     local_port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(((struct sockaddr_in6 *)&local_addr)->sin6_port);
678   }
679   g_my_client_id = (uint32_t)local_port;
680 
681   // Mark connection as active immediately after successful socket connection
682   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
683   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
684   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_reconnect, false);
685 
686   // Initialize crypto BEFORE starting protocol handshake
687   // Note: server IP is already set above in the connection loop
688   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_init()");
689   if (client_crypto_init() != 0) {
690     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto (password required or incorrect)");
691     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_init() failed");
692     close_socket(g_sockfd);
693     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
694     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED; // SSH key password was wrong - no retry
695   }
696 
697   // Create the persistent TCP transport before handshake.
698   // It starts with NULL crypto; client_crypto_handshake() sets crypto_ctx on success.
699   g_client_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("client", g_sockfd, NULL);
700   if (!g_client_transport) {
701     log_error("Failed to create TCP ACIP transport");
702     close_socket(g_sockfd);
703     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
704     return -1;
705   }
706   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Created TCP ACIP transport (crypto pending handshake)");
707 
708   // Perform crypto handshake if encryption is enabled
709   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_handshake()");
710   int handshake_result = client_crypto_handshake(g_client_transport);
711   if (handshake_result != 0) {
712     log_error("Crypto handshake failed");
713     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_handshake() failed with code %d", handshake_result);
714     acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
715     g_client_transport = NULL;
716     close_socket(g_sockfd);
717     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
718     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE,
719           "Crypto handshake failed with server - this usually indicates a protocol mismatch or network issue");
720   }
721   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_handshake() succeeded");
722 
723   // Turn OFF terminal logging when successfully connected to server
724   // First connection - we'll disable logging after main.c shows the "Connected successfully" message
725   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
726     log_debug("Connected to server - terminal logging will be disabled after initial setup");
727   } else {
728     log_debug("Connected to server - terminal logging kept enabled for snapshot mode");
729   }
730 
731   // Configure socket options for optimal performance
732   if (socket_set_keepalive(g_sockfd, true) < 0) {
733     log_warn("Failed to set socket keepalive: %s", network_error_string());
734   }
735 
736   // Configure socket buffers and TCP_NODELAY for optimal performance
737   asciichat_error_t sock_config_result = socket_configure_buffers(g_sockfd);
738   if (sock_config_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
739     log_warn("Failed to configure socket: %s", network_error_string());
740   }
741 
742   // Send initial terminal capabilities to server (this may generate debug logs)
743   int result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
744                                                             (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
745   if (result < 0) {
746     log_error("Failed to send initial capabilities to server: %s", network_error_string());
747     close_socket(g_sockfd);
748     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
749     return -1;
750   }
751 
752   // Now disable terminal logging after capabilities are sent (for reconnections)
753   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && has_ever_connected) {
754     log_set_terminal_output(false);
755     log_debug("Reconnected to server - terminal logging disabled to prevent interference with ASCII display");
756   }
757 
758   // Send client join packet for multi-user support
759   uint32_t my_capabilities = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO; // Basic video capability
760   log_debug("GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) = %d (sending CLIENT_JOIN)", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
761   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
762     log_debug("Adding CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO to capabilities");
763     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO;
764   }
765   if (GET_OPTION(color_mode) != COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
766     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_COLOR;
767   }
768   if (GET_OPTION(stretch)) {
769     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
770   }
771 
772   // Generate display name from username + PID
773   const char *display_name = platform_get_username();
774 
775   char my_display_name[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
776   int pid = getpid();
777   SAFE_SNPRINTF(my_display_name, sizeof(my_display_name), "%s-%d", display_name, pid);
778 
779   if (threaded_send_client_join_packet(my_display_name, my_capabilities) < 0) {
780     log_error("Failed to send client join packet: %s", network_error_string());
781     close_socket(g_sockfd);
782     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
783     return -1;
784   }
785 
786   // Connection already marked as active after socket creation
787 
788   return 0;
789 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(),
    acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO,
    CLIENT_CAP_COLOR, CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH, CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO,
    client_crypto_handshake(), client_crypto_init(),
    close_socket, COLOR_MODE_NONE, CONNECT_TIMEOUT,
    connect_with_timeout(), CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED,
    crypto_client_get_context(), crypto_client_is_ready(),
    ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE, FATAL, format_ip_address(),
    GET_OPTION, url_parts_t::host, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,

    log_error, log_info, log_set_terminal_output(),

int
  server_connection_init ()


Initialize the server connection management subsystem. Initialize
    server connection management subsystem.




Sets up the send mutex and initializes connection state variables.
    Must be called once during client startup before any connection
  attempts.




Returns


0 on success, non-zero on failure

310   // Initialize mutex for thread-safe packet sending

314   }
315 
316   // Register connection atomics for debug sync state monitoring (one-time registration)
317   static bool connection_atomics_registered = false;
318   if (!connection_atomics_registered) {
319     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_connection_active, "server_connection_active_flag", NULL);
320     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_connection_lost, "server_connection_lost_flag", NULL);
321     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_reconnect, "should_attempt_reconnection_flag", NULL);
322     connection_atomics_registered = true;
323   }
324 
325   // Initialize connection state
326   g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
327   g_client_transport = NULL;

void
  server_connection_shutdown ()


Emergency connection shutdown for signal handlers. Emergency
    shutdown for signal handlers.




Performs immediate connection shutdown without waiting for
    graceful close procedures. Uses socket shutdown to interrupt any blocking
    recv() operations in other threads.


Definition at line 1042 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1042                                   {
1043   // NOTE: This function may be called from:
1044   //   - Signal handlers on Unix (async-signal-safe context)
1045   //   - SetConsoleCtrlHandler callback thread on Windows (separate thread context)
1046   // Only use atomic operations and simple system calls - NO mutex locks, NO malloc, NO logging.
1047 
1048   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
1049   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, true);
1050 
1051   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1052     // Only shutdown() the socket to interrupt blocking recv()/send() operations.
1053     // Do NOT close() here - on Windows, closing the socket while another thread
1054     // is using it is undefined behavior and can cause STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN.
1055     // The actual socket close happens in server_connection_close() which is called
1056     // from the main thread after worker threads have been joined.
1057     socket_shutdown(g_sockfd, SHUT_RDWR);
1058   }
1059 
1060   // For WebSocket transport: interrupt blocking recv() calls
1061   // The recv() checks is_connected before waiting for data. Setting this to false
1062   // will cause any blocked recv() to return immediately with CONNECTION_CLOSED.
1063   // We use a function that's async-signal-safe (no mutexes) to do this.
1064   server_connection_interrupt_websocket_recv();
1065 
1066   // DO NOT call log_set_terminal_output() here - it uses mutex which is NOT async-signal-safe.
1067   // The normal cleanup path in shutdown_client() will handle logging state.
1068 }

1185 
1186   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1187   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1188     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1189     return -1;
1190   }
1191 
1192   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1193   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1194 
1195   // Network I/O happens while holding mutex to prevent concurrent socket writes
1196   asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_audio_batch(transport, samples, (uint32_t)num_samples, (uint32_t)batch_count);
1197 
1198   // Unlock after send completes
1199   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1200

asciichat_error_t threaded_send_audio_opus (const
  uint8_t * opus_data, size_t opus_size, int sample_rate, int
  frame_duration)


Thread-safe Opus audio frame transmission. Sends a single
    Opus-encoded audio frame to the server with proper synchronization and
    encryption support.




Parameters


opus_data Opus-encoded audio data


opus_size Size of encoded frame


sample_rate Sample rate in Hz


frame_duration Frame duration in milliseconds


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1224 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1225                                                                {
1226   // Get transport reference while holding mutex (brief lock)
1227   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1228 
1229   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1230   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1231     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1232     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
1233   }
1234 
1235   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1236   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1237   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1238 
1239   // Build Opus packet with header (outside mutex - no blocking I/O yet)
1240   size_t header_size = 16; // sample_rate (4), frame_duration (4), reserved (8)
1241   size_t total_size = header_size + opus_size;
1242   void *packet_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, total_size);
1243   if (!packet_data) {
1244     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for Opus packet: %zu bytes", total_size);
1245   }
1246 
1247   // Write header in network byte order
1248   uint8_t *buf = (uint8_t *)packet_data;
1249   uint32_t sr = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)sample_rate);
1250   uint32_t fd = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)frame_duration);
1251   memcpy(buf, &sr, 4);
1252   memcpy(buf + 4, &fd, 4);
1253   memset(buf + 8, 0, 8); // Reserved
1254 
1255   // Copy Opus data
1256   memcpy(buf + header_size, opus_data, opus_size);
1257 
1258   // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex to prevent deadlock on TCP buffer full
1259   asciichat_error_t result =
1260       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH, packet_data, total_size, 0);
1261 
1262   // Clean up
1263   buffer_pool_free(NULL, packet_data, total_size);
1264 
1265   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1266   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1267     server_connection_lost();
1268     return result;
1269   }
1270 
1271   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1272 }

    mutex_unlock, packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH, server_connection_lost(), and
    SET_ERRNO.

1617   // Build CLIENT_JOIN packet locally
1618   client_info_packet_t join_packet;
1619   SAFE_MEMSET(&join_packet, sizeof(join_packet), 0, sizeof(join_packet));
1620   join_packet.client_id = HOST_TO_NET_U32(0); // Will be assigned by server
1621   SAFE_SNPRINTF(join_packet.display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN, "%s", display_name ? display_name : "Unknown");
1622   join_packet.capabilities = HOST_TO_NET_U32(capabilities);
1623 
1624   // Use threaded_send_packet() which handles encryption
1625   int send_result = threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_JOIN, &join_packet, sizeof(join_packet));
1626   if (send_result == 0) {
1627     mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1628     bool active = atomic_load_u64(&g_connection_active);
1629     socket_t socket_snapshot = g_sockfd;
1630     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
1631     if (active && socket_snapshot != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1632       (void)log_network_message(
1633           socket_snapshot, (const struct crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx, LOG_INFO, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER,
1634           "CLIENT_JOIN sent (display=\"%s\", capabilities=0x%x)", join_packet.display_name, capabilities);
1635     }
1636     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1637   }
1638   return send_result;
1639 }

asciichat_error_t threaded_send_image_frame (const void *
  pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height, uint32_t
  pixel_format)


Thread-safe image frame transmission. Sends image frames with
    serialization via g_send_mutex to prevent race conditions when multiple
    threads write to the same TCP socket.




Parameters


pixel_data Pixel data buffer


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


pixel_format Pixel format (1=RGB24)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1333 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1334                                                                    {
1335   if (!pixel_data) {
1336     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Pixel data is NULL");
1337   }
1338 
1339   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1340 
1341   // Get transport reference - may be NULL if connection was lost
1342   acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1343   if (!transport || !server_connection_is_active()) {
1344     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1345     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport unavailable");
1346   }
1347 
1348   // Send image frame with socket lock held
1349   asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_image_frame(transport, pixel_data, width, height, pixel_format);
1350 
1351   // Unlock after send completes
1352   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1353 
1354   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1355   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1356     server_connection_lost();
1357     return result;
1358   }
1359 
1360   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1361 }

    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), server_connection_lost(), and
    SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t threaded_send_image_frame_h265 (const
  void * pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height)


Send H.265-encoded video frame to server (thread-safe). Encodes
    RGB frame using H.265 codec before transmission. Much more
    bandwidth-efficient than raw pixel transmission. Uses mutex to prevent
    interleaving with other packet sends.




Parameters


pixel_data RGB pixel data from camera


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1377 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1377                                                                                                           {
1378   if (!pixel_data) {
1379     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Pixel data is NULL");
1380   }
1381 
1382   // Get encoder - may be NULL if not initialized
1383   h265_encoder_t *encoder = g_h265_encoder;
1384   if (!encoder) {
1385     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "H.265 encoder not initialized");
1386   }
1387 
1388   // Allocate buffer and encode frame WITHOUT holding the send mutex.
1389   // This avoids long critical section that can cause deadlocks with named registry operations.
1390   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_1] Allocating buffer and encoding frame (no mutex held)");
1391   size_t output_buffer_size = 5 + (width * height * 2);
1392   uint8_t *output_buffer = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, output_buffer_size);
1393   if (!output_buffer) {
1394     log_error("Failed to allocate output buffer for frame");
1395     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate H.265 output buffer: %zu bytes", output_buffer_size);
1396   }
1397 
1398   // Encode frame
1399   size_t encoded_size = output_buffer_size - 5;
1400   asciichat_error_t encode_result = h265_encode(encoder, (uint16_t)width, (uint16_t)height, (const uint8_t *)pixel_data,
1401                                                 output_buffer + 5, &encoded_size);
1402   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_2] ✅ Frame encoded, encoded_size=%zu, result=%d", encoded_size, encode_result);
1403 
1404   if (encode_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1405     log_error("H.265 encoding failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(encode_result));
1406     buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
1407     return encode_result;
1408   }
1409 
1410   // Prepare packet header: [flags:u8][width:u16][height:u16][h265_data...]
1411   output_buffer[0] = 0; // flags: no flags set by default
1412   output_buffer[1] = (width >> 8) & 0xFF;
1413   output_buffer[2] = width & 0xFF;
1414   output_buffer[3] = (height >> 8) & 0xFF;
1415   output_buffer[4] = height & 0xFF;
1416 
1417   size_t total_size = 5 + encoded_size;
1418 
1419   // NOW acquire the send mutex just for the actual network transmission
1420   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_3] About to acquire g_send_mutex for network send");
1421   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1422   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_4] ✅ Acquired g_send_mutex, checking transport");
1423 
1424   // Get transport reference - may be NULL if connection was lost
1425   acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1426   if (!transport || !server_connection_is_active()) {
1427     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1428     log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_5] Transport unavailable, releasing mutex");
1429     buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
1430     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport unavailable");
1431   }
1432 
1433   // Send H.265 frame with socket lock held (short critical section)
1434   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_6] Sending packet via transport (%zu bytes)", total_size);
1435   asciichat_error_t result =
1436       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_IMAGE_FRAME_H265, output_buffer, total_size, 0);
1437   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_7] packet_send_via_transport returned: %d", result);
1438 
1439   // Unlock after send completes
1440   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1441   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_8] ✅ Released g_send_mutex");
1442 
1443   buffer_pool_free(NULL, output_buffer, output_buffer_size);
1444   log_info("[H265_SEND_FRAME_9] ✅ Freed output buffer");
1445 
1446   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1447   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1448     server_connection_lost();
1449     return result;
1450   }
1451 
1452   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1453 }

asciichat_error_t threaded_send_packet
  (packet_type_t type, const void * data, size_t len)


Thread-safe packet transmission. Sends a packet to the server with
    proper mutex protection and connection state checking. Automatically handles
    encryption if crypto is ready.




Parameters


type Packet type identifier


data Packet payload


len Payload length


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1135 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1135                                                                                          {
1136   // Lock mutex for entire send operation to prevent concurrent socket writes
1137   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1138 
1139   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1140   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1141     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() check failed: active=%s, transport=%p",
1142               atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) ? "true" : "false", (void *)g_client_transport);
1143     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1144     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
1145   }
1146 
1147   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1148   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1149   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() using transport=%p, is_connected=%s",
1150                   (void *)transport, acip_transport_is_connected(transport) ? "true" : "false");
1151 
1152   // Network I/O happens while holding mutex to prevent concurrent socket writes
1153   asciichat_error_t result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, type, data, len, 0);
1154 
1155   // Unlock after send completes
1156   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1157 
1158   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1159   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1160     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() send failed, calling server_connection_lost()");
1161     server_connection_lost();
1162     return result;
1163   }
1164 
1165   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1166 }

    ERROR_NETWORK, log_debug, log_debug_every,
    LOG_RATE_SLOW, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    packet_send_via_transport(), server_connection_lost(), and
    SET_ERRNO.

  threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect (unsigned short width, unsigned

Thread-safe terminal size packet transmission with auto-detection.

    capabilities if not explicitly specified.




Parameters


width Terminal width in characters


height Terminal height in characters


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1510 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1510                                                                                                             {
1511   // Log the dimensions being sent to server (helps debug dimension mismatch issues)
1512   log_debug("Sending terminal size to server: %ux%u (auto_width=%d, auto_height=%d)", width, height,
1513             GET_OPTION(auto_width), GET_OPTION(auto_height));
1514 
1515   // Connection and transport availability is checked by threaded_send_packet()
1516 
1517   // Build terminal capabilities packet locally
1518   // Detect terminal capabilities automatically
1519   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1520 
1521   // Set wants_padding based on snapshot mode and TTY status
1522   // Disable padding when:
1523   // - In snapshot mode (one frame and exit)
1524   // - When stdout is not a TTY (piped/redirected output)
1525   // Enable padding for interactive terminal sessions
1526   bool is_snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
1527   bool is_interactive = terminal_is_interactive();
1528   caps.wants_padding = is_interactive && !is_snapshot_mode;
1529 
1530   log_debug("Client capabilities: wants_padding=%d (snapshot=%d, interactive=%d, stdin_tty=%d, stdout_tty=%d)",
1531             caps.wants_padding, is_snapshot_mode, is_interactive, terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty());
1532 
1533   // Apply user's color mode override
1534   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
1535 
1536   // Check if detection was reliable, use fallback only for auto-detection
1537   if (!caps.detection_reliable && GET_OPTION(color_mode) == COLOR_MODE_AUTO) {
1538     log_warn("Terminal capability detection not reliable, using fallback");
1539     SAFE_MEMSET(&caps, sizeof(caps), 0, sizeof(caps));
1540     caps.color_level = TERM_COLOR_NONE;
1541     caps.color_count = 2;
1542     caps.capabilities = 0;
1543     SAFE_STRNCPY(caps.term_type, "unknown", sizeof(caps.term_type));
1544     SAFE_STRNCPY(caps.colorterm, "", sizeof(caps.colorterm));
1545     caps.detection_reliable = 0;
1546     // Preserve wants_padding even in fallback mode
1547     caps.wants_padding = is_interactive && !is_snapshot_mode;
1548   }
1549 
1550   // Convert to network packet format with proper byte order
1551   terminal_capabilities_packet_t net_packet;
1552   net_packet.capabilities = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.capabilities);
1553   net_packet.color_level = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.color_level);
1554   net_packet.color_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.color_count);
1555   net_packet.render_mode = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.render_mode);
1556   net_packet.width = HOST_TO_NET_U16(width);
1557   net_packet.height = HOST_TO_NET_U16(height);
1558   net_packet.palette_type = HOST_TO_NET_U32(GET_OPTION(palette_type));
1559   net_packet.utf8_support = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.utf8_support ? 1 : 0);
1560 
1561   const options_t *opts = options_get();
1562   if (GET_OPTION(palette_type) == PALETTE_CUSTOM && GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set)) {
1563     const char *palette_custom = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : "";
1564     SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.palette_custom, palette_custom, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom));
1565     net_packet.palette_custom[sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom) - 1] = '\0';
1566   } else {
1567     SAFE_MEMSET(net_packet.palette_custom, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom), 0, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom));
1568   }
1569 
1570   // Set desired FPS
1571   int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
1572   if (fps > 0) {
1573     net_packet.desired_fps = (uint8_t)(fps > 144 ? 144 : fps);
1574   } else {
1575     net_packet.desired_fps = caps.desired_fps;
1576   }
1577 
1578   if (net_packet.desired_fps == 0) {
1579     net_packet.desired_fps = DEFAULT_MAX_FPS;
1580   }
1581 
1582   SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.term_type, caps.term_type, sizeof(net_packet.term_type));
1583   net_packet.term_type[sizeof(net_packet.term_type) - 1] = '\0';
1584 
1585   SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.colorterm, caps.colorterm, sizeof(net_packet.colorterm));
1586   net_packet.colorterm[sizeof(net_packet.colorterm) - 1] = '\0';
1587 
1588   net_packet.detection_reliable = caps.detection_reliable;
1589   // Send UTF-8 support flag: true for AUTO (default) and TRUE settings, false for FALSE setting
1590   net_packet.utf8_support = (GET_OPTION(force_utf8) != UTF8_SETTING_FALSE) ? 1 : 0;
1591 
1592   // Set wants_padding flag (1=padding enabled, 0=no padding for snapshot/piped modes)
1593   net_packet.wants_padding = caps.wants_padding ? 1 : 0;
1594 
1595   // Set codec capabilities (client supports all codecs by default)
1596   net_packet.codec_capabilities_video = HOST_TO_NET_U32(VIDEO_CODEC_CAP_ALL);
1597   net_packet.codec_capabilities_audio = HOST_TO_NET_U32(AUDIO_CODEC_CAP_ALL);
1598 
1599   // Use threaded_send_packet() which handles encryption
1600   return threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES, &net_packet, sizeof(net_packet));
1601 }

Per-connection crypto handshake context. Global crypto handshake

Global H.265 encoder context for client video streaming. Encodes
